name: Cerro de la Mojonera Trail
name_original: Sendero del Cerro de la Mojonera (PRC-BU 176) — circular desde Pardilla
description: Hike the moderate 7.0-mile Cerro de la Mojonera Trail near Pardilla, Spain, gaining 529 feet over varied terrain in about 3 hours.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Pardilla are primarily found within the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park, characterized by deep fissures carved into calcareous rocks by the Riaza River. The landscape features limestone canyons and the river course, offering varied terrain for outdoor activities. This protected area is also known as a significant raptor refuge. Best hiking trails around Pardilla The most popular hiking route is Natural Park of the Hoces de Rio Riaza — circular from Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This circular path explores the natural park's distinct riverine and canyon features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Senda de la Ermita de Casuar — circular desde Valdevacas de Montejo: Parque natural Hoces del río Riaza, a moderate 6.8 miles (10.9 km) path. This route offers views of the Riaza River and its surrounding limestone formations. Local hikers also love the Hoces del Riaza — circular desde Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela: Natural Park Hoces del río Riaza, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail leading through the Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Hiking around Pardilla is defined by limestone canyons, the Riaza River, and deep fissures, with trails suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Pardilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 11 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Pardilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Pardilla?
A: Hiking around Pardilla is characterized by the stunning landscape of the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park. You'll find deep fissures carved into calcareous (limestone) rocks by the Riaza River, creating dramatic limestone canyons and river courses. The trails offer varied terrain, from riverside paths to routes through the canyons.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Pardilla area?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around Pardilla, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and a few more challenging options to explore the natural beauty of the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Pardilla?
A: Yes, the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park offers several easy, flat options suitable for families. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153664073/hoces-del-riaza-circular-desde-montejo-de-la-vega-de-la-serrezuela-natural-park-hoces-del-rio-riaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Riaza — circular desde Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.6 miles (7.4 km) long and can be completed in under two hours. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036633622/sendero-del-arroyo-de-la-serrezuela-circular-desde-aldehorno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Arroyo de la Serrezuela — circular desde Aldehorno&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Pardilla?
A: While the region is generally welcoming to outdoor activities, specific regulations for dogs within the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park may apply, especially concerning wildlife protection. It's always best to check local signage or contact the Casa del Parque for the most current information regarding dog access on trails.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking in Pardilla?
A: The Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park is a significant raptor refuge, hosting one of the largest colonies of griffon vultures in Europe, with approximately 150 pairs. Keep an eye on the skies for these magnificent birds. The park's protected status ensures a rich biodiversity, making it an excellent spot for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Pardilla?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Pardilla are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036582116/natural-park-of-the-hoces-de-rio-riaza-circular-from-montejo-de-la-vega-de-la-serrezuela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Hoces de Rio Riaza — circular from Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.3-mile (13.3 km) trail. Another favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153647135/senda-de-la-ermita-de-casuar-circular-desde-valdevacas-de-montejo-parque-natural-hoces-del-rio-riaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Ermita de Casuar — circular desde Valdevacas de Montejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Do I need a permit to hike certain trails in the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park?
A: Yes, some trails within the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park may require special permits during certain periods, specifically from January 1 to July 31. This is due to bird breeding seasons and conservation efforts. You can find information and obtain necessary authorizations at the Casa del Parque.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the hiking trails?
A: The area around Pardilla offers both natural and cultural attractions. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5327014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of the Hoces del Río Riaza&lt;/a&gt;, or visit historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5327016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Martín del Casuar&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5623538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Hornuez&lt;/a&gt;. The impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2557040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaduct of the Madrid–Burgos Railway over the Riaza Gorge&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable sight.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Pardilla?
A: The hiking trails around Pardilla are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from 11 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning limestone canyons, the presence of the Riaza River, and the diverse terrain suitable for various ability levels.

Q: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the hiking areas?
A: The region offers cultural richness with picturesque cobblestone streets in nearby municipalities like Maderuelo. While specific establishments aren't listed, these towns typically provide local amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation, allowing you to relax and refuel after your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Pardilla?
A: The Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park offers scenic beauty year-round. However, be aware that some trails may require special permits from January 1 to July 31 due to bird breeding seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking, while summer can be warm. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.
tips: Town of Burgos in which the Parish Church stands out, which preserves a part of the 12th century, although it was mostly restored in the 16th and 17th centuries.

faqs.question: Where is the Cerro de la Mojonera Trail located?
faqs.answer: This specific route, the Cerro de la Mojonera Trail, starts and ends in the village of Pardilla, which is located in the province of Burgos, Castile and León, Spain. It's important to note that there are other trails with similar names in different regions of Spain and Mexico, but this particular komoot tour is centered around Pardilla.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: The Cerro de la Mojonera Trail is rated as moderate. With a distance of approximately 11.3 kilometers and an elevation gain of around 161 meters, it offers a good challenge for hikers with some experience.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the trail?
faqs.answer: On average, hikers can expect to complete the Cerro de la Mojonera Trail in about 3 hours and 5 minutes. This duration can vary based on individual pace, breaks, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating, the trail is best suited for hikers with some prior experience. While not overly strenuous, its length and elevation gain might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro de la Mojonera Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route for the Cerro de la Mojonera Trail is a circular loop, starting and ending in Pardilla.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While specific local details are not available, trails in the Castile and León region are generally pleasant for hiking during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring cold temperatures and snow, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on hiking trails in rural areas of Spain, especially if kept under control or on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead for any specific restrictions regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information suggesting that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike the Cerro de la Mojonera Trail near Pardilla. Most public hiking trails in Spain are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in the village of Pardilla, parking would typically be available within or on the outskirts of the village. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots that do not obstruct local traffic or private property.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: While specific details for this exact route are not provided, trails in the Burgos region often feature a mix of dirt paths, gravel tracks, and potentially some rocky sections, traversing agricultural lands, forests, and hills. The moderate difficulty suggests varied terrain.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cerro de la Mojonera Trail route incorporates sections of several other official trails, including the Sendero del Cerro de la Mojonera, Circuito Permantente Memorial Alberto Fernández, Cañada Real Segoviana, Reguero del Val, and Camino del Hocino.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or notable features along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail's name, 'Cerro de la Mojonera,' suggests it likely ascends to a 'cerro' (hill) which often provides panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. The route starts and ends in the charming village of Pardilla, offering a glimpse into local life.
